title: lobelia polyphylla
intro: Lobelia polyphylla, also known as the Kamahi or New Zealand Lobelia, is a perennial plant native to New Zealand, characterized by its green, fleshy leaves and small, bell-shaped flowers. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Lobelia polyphylla include its ability to relieve respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce stress and anxiety. In herbal preparations, Lobelia polyphylla is often combined with other herbs and used in teas, tinctures, and capsules to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
